signals.title: USD/JPY — Intervention Risk + Carry + Positioning
signals.text: USD/JPY at 160.35 — Japanese Yen round-trips Tokyo's record intervention level, with 160.00 having previously triggered BoJ warnings. The 290bp Fed-BoJ OIS carry premium (SOFR 3.63% vs TONA 0.73%) sustains the bid, but intervention risk is acute on any sustained move above 160.00. JPY COT LF net short −105,136 (crowded carry trade). Watch for BoJ verbal warnings or direct action if price extends higher; next BoJ meeting (mkt: 50% hike priced) on 16 Jun is a key catalyst.

signals.title: AUD/USD — G10 Bilateral Theme + Geopolitical + CESI
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7027 — AUD is the weakest G10 currency this week (avg -0.944% vs peers), while USD is the strongest (avg +0.714%), creating a 1.658% spread. This aligns with the headline that the Australian Dollar tests a six-week low as Trump rattles markets with Middle East war threats. AUD CESI net -8 (data disappointing) further supports AUD weakness. The 72bp AUD carry premium (AONIA 4.35% vs SOFR 3.63%) is being overwhelmed by risk-off flows and USD strength. Watch for further geopolitical escalation or US CPI data for sustained AUD pressure.

signals.title: USD/CHF — Volatility Premium + Geopolitical Risk
signals.text: USD/CHF at 0.7991 — IV at 34.5% vs HV30 at 6.6% results in an extreme +27.9% VRP, indicating strong hedging demand for CHF protection. This elevated options premium is driven by Middle East war escalation headlines (Trump threatens war over a helicopter) and general risk aversion, despite VIX at 19.9 (normal volatility). The 375bp Fed-SNB carry premium (Fed 3.75% vs SNB 0.0%) supports USD, but safe-haven demand for CHF is driving options skew. Watch for further geopolitical developments to confirm CHF safe-haven bid.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs Retail Divergence + Extreme Retail Long
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5822 — COT LF net short −27,797 (specs are significantly short), while retail sentiment shows an extreme 88% long bias. This institutional vs. retail divergence suggests retail traders are fading the speculative short. If price extends lower, retail capitulation risk is elevated, potentially fueling further downside. The 150bp USD carry premium (Fed 3.75% vs RBNZ 2.25%) structurally favors USD. Watch for a break below 0.5800 to confirm retail capitulation.
